text:
Once upon a time, there lived a grumpy old turtle who'd retired from chasing after lost carrots. Every dawn, he'd creep out of his comfy shell to keep an eye on the sunrise from his little observatory. One particular day, as the sky was ablaze with pink and orange, he saw something incredible—an odd-looking bird fluttering toward his territory.

Curious, the turtle watched as the bird landed beside him. It had feathers unlike anything he'd ever seen. The bird spoke in soft, melodic tones.

"I've traveled far and wide to find someone with an affinity for shells. Tell me, what makes your little home so special?"

The turtle considered this for a moment and replied with a scowl. "A carrot... it's tasty."

"Ah," the bird chirped softly. "Well then, how about a carrot party tonight? I've been cultivating a particularly sweet variety."

The turtle's nose wrinkled. "*Great*. If it's for carrots?"
